channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Kane Brown’s Got a ‘Woman,’ And a Reinvigorated Enthusiasm for His Career: ‘A New Era of Music For Me’
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: Welcome to our podcast, where we dive into the latest music trends. Today, we're talking about country star Kane Brown, who's just signed a new deal with RCA Records and is kicking off a new era of music. Kane Brown has come a long way since his YouTube days, posting covers of 80s and 90s country songs. He's now an arena-level headliner, and his new single "Woman" is making waves on country radio.
[Music clip: "Woman" by Kane Brown]
Host: We caught up with Kane to talk about his new music and what inspired him to get back into the studio. He says he's excited to be back, after a few years of feeling unenthusiastic about his craft. He credits getting back into boxing and finding his competitive spirit again for reigniting his passion for music.
Kane Brown [audio clip]: "I got lucky with some songs like 'Backseat Driver' and 'Miles on It', but I wasn't excited. I got back into boxing, got my competitiveness and excitement back, and just got off my butt, not being lazy anymore. I'm excited to be back in the studio, writing songs and getting back on the road."
Host: Kane's new single "Woman" has been a hit with his fans, and it's easy to see why. The track has a high-energy vibe, reminiscent of classic hits from Bryan Adams to Shania Twain. Kane says he knew from the moment he wrote the song that it would be a crowd-pleaser.
Kane Brown [audio clip]: "I feel like you could listen to this song and play Shania Twain's '(Man!) I Feel Like a Woman!' and then [Cyndi Lauper's] 'Girls Just Want to Have Fun' and [Whitney Houston's] 'I Want to Dance with Somebody'. I feel like it fits in with that."
Host: "Woman" was written during a songwriting retreat in Nashville, with some of Kane's favorite co-writers. The idea came to him when he was thinking about his wife and how much he'd rather spend time with her than go out with his friends.
[Outro music plays]
Host: Thanks for tuning in to our podcast. Kane Brown's new single "Woman" is out now, and we can't wait to see what's next for this country star.
